title: Observation of a singular Weyl point surrounded by charged nodal walls in PtGa
type1_summary: Both files are Nature source-data PDFs with sizes consistent with figure-oriented supplemental data, and the paper context supports source/processed data deposits for replotting.
type2_summary: No file shows evidence of raw instrument output, proprietary binary formats, or large unprocessed measurement data.
classification_notes: The archive likely contains source-data supplements rather than raw experimental files. Because the PDFs were not content-inspected, the classification is based on metadata, paper context, and Nature source-data conventions.

With no evidence of proprietary/raw instrument structure, the conservative classification is Type 1.

paper_analysis.summary: This paper reports ARPES and spin-resolved ARPES measurements, supported by DFT/Wannier-based calculations, showing that PtGa hosts a singular Weyl point at the Brillouin-zone center surrounded by symmetry-protected charged nodal walls on the zone boundaries. The key result is the experimental observation of an unpaired Weyl point with no corresponding surface Fermi arc, plus additional accidental Weyl points, extending the usual Nielsen–Ninomiya pairing picture to higher-dimensional topological objects.

figures.description: Schematic of a conventional Weyl semimetal obeying the no-go theorem, with paired Weyl points and surface Fermi arcs connecting opposite chiral charges.
Schematic of a topological semimetal with high-chirality Weyl fermions in the CoSi family, showing large Fermi arcs connecting bulk Weyl nodes at high-symmetry points.
Schematic of the proposed singular Weyl semimetal: a single Weyl point at the BZ center enclosed by Weyl nodal walls on the BZ boundaries, with no surface Fermi arc.
Three-dimensional bulk Brillouin zone of PtGa.
Crystal/unit-cell structure of PtGa.
Calculated bulk band structure of PtGa along high-symmetry lines, highlighting the singular Weyl point at Γ and doubly degenerate Weyl nodal walls at the BZ boundaries.
ARPES Fermi-surface map in the Γ–M–X plane at hv = 605 eV, showing bulk and surface features including large Fermi arcs.
ARPES Fermi-surface map in the X–R–M plane at hv = 545 eV, showing bulk and surface features.
ARPES Fermi-surface map in the Γ–X–M plane at hv = 605 eV and 12 K.
High-resolution ARPES spectrum along Γ–X showing the Weyl point near Γ.
Curvature-enhanced version of the Γ–X ARPES spectrum to highlight band crossings.
Calculated band dispersion along X–Γ–X, used to compare with ARPES and identify the singular Weyl point.
High-resolution ARPES spectrum along Γ–M in the second Brillouin zone.
Curvature-enhanced Γ–M ARPES spectrum.
Calculated band dispersion along M–Γ–M.
3D intensity plot of the electronic structure in the Γ–X–M plane, marking the singular Weyl point and the Weyl nodal walls on the BZ boundary.
Alternating ARPES spectra and calculated dispersions along multiple BZ-boundary high-symmetry lines (MX1, MX2, MR, XR), demonstrating the nodal walls.
figures.data_type: ARPES spectra and band structure plots
figures.likely_source: raw measurement for ARPES; derived for calculations

figures.description: 3D band structure along R–M–X showing Fermi-surface pockets with nonzero Chern number near M.
3D band structure along Γ–X–M showing Fermi-surface pockets with nonzero Chern number near M.
Spin-integrated ARPES band structure along Γ–M near the singular Weyl point, with MDC/EDC cut positions marked.
Calculated Berry-curvature field components on the Fermi-surface pockets formed by bands N−3 and N−2.
Spin-resolved ARPES MDC spectra projected onto X, Y, and Z spin directions.
Spin-resolved ARPES EDC spectra projected onto X, Y, and Z spin directions.
Schematic grouping of 6 accidental Weyl points and their symmetry-equivalent partners in momentum space, plus the central Weyl point.
Symmetry-generated full set of Weyl points in the Brillouin zone.
Projections of the Weyl points along crystallographic directions, indicating which points lie in the chosen plane.
Symmetrized ARPES spectrum and curvature plot along cut1 across two WP1 points.
Calculated band structure along cut1.
Raw ARPES spectrum and curvature plot along cut1 across two WP1 points.
In-plane Berry-curvature field in the kz = 0 plane with projected Weyl points and nodal-wall charge distribution overlaid.
ARPES spectrum and curvature plot along cut2 across two WP2 points.
ARPES spectrum along cut2 in the second Brillouin zone.
Calculated band structure along cut2.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: The paper reports raw ARPES and spin-resolved ARPES measurements on PtGa single crystals, including Fermi-surface maps, high-resolution band dispersions, MDC/EDC spectra, and spin-resolved intensity spectra. It also reports crystal characterization measurements using white-beam backscattering Laue X-ray and single-crystal X-ray diffraction.
paper_analysis.processed_plot_details: The paper includes curvature-intensity plots of ARPES spectra, symmetrized spectra, 3D intensity plots, Berry-curvature field visualizations, and calculated band structures/topological analyses from DFT and WannierTools. These are processed/derived representations of the measured or computed electronic structure.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: Data supporting the conclusions are available in a public repository (MARVEL Materials Cloud Archive) with the same title of the paper.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: The paper states that supporting data are in a public repository, but it does not specify file formats, folder structure, or whether the repository contains raw detector outputs. Raw measurements were collected by ARPES/SARPES at several synchrotron beamlines and by X-ray characterization, but the paper text does not describe raw file formats.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include curvature plots, symmetrized ARPES cuts, extracted band dispersions, Berry-curvature maps, and calculated band structures/topological quantities (Weyl point positions, chiralities, Chern-related analysis). The paper also mentions Supplementary Tables listing Weyl point coordinates, energies, and chiralities.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Figure-specific source data are implied by the public archive statement, but the paper text does not explicitly say 'source data' or describe per-figure files. The figures themselves are a mix of raw ARPES images and derived plots.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: Analysis software is mentioned: VASP for DFT, Wannier90 for Wannier tight-binding construction, and WannierTools for Weyl-point location and chirality calculation. No custom analysis scripts or notebooks are mentioned.
dataset_characterization.replot_reason: The paper indicates that supporting data are deposited in a public archive, and the figures include many directly plottable spectra and calculated curves. However, the text does not specify whether the archive contains raw detector data, figure source data, or scripts, so replot readiness is likely but not fully verifiable from the paper text alone.
dataset_characterization.notes: The dataset is a mixture of experimental ARPES/SARPES measurements and theoretical calculations. The paper clearly distinguishes raw spectra from processed curvature/symmetrized plots and from derived DFT/Wannier results. Supplementary material is referenced for additional figures, tables, and comparisons, including a table of 37 Weyl points and supplementary figures for alternative light polarizations and candidate materials.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: The Methods section explicitly describes ARPES, SARPES, and X-ray measurements, which generate raw experimental data.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: The paper contains many processed/derived figure panels such as curvature intensity plots, symmetrized spectra, Berry-curvature maps, and calculated band structures; it also states that data are available in a public archive.
classification_prior.both_evidence: The paper combines raw experimental spectra with processed figure panels and theoretical outputs, so both raw and source/processed data are likely present in the archive, though only the existence of the archive is explicitly stated.
data_availability_section_relevant: Data that support the conclusions of this study are available in a public repository (MARVEL Materials Cloud Archive) with the same title of this paper
The energy and angular resolutions were set to 5–100 meV and 0.1°
The spin-resolved ARPES measurements were performed at the COPHEE end station
The location of WPs and the calculation of their chirality have been performed using the open-source code WannierTools based on the Wannier tight-binding model constructed using the Wannier90 code
classification_prior.review_notes: Be careful not to equate the public archive with raw data only: the paper text supports a mix of raw measurements and derived/processed outputs, but does not explicitly enumerate repository contents. Source data are implied by figure-based results, but not explicitly labeled as such in the text.
